series_metadata.series_description: Including any supporting documentation created and/or used to justify and support legislative appropriations requests by the university.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Any document that officially initiates, rescinds, or amends general office procedures.
Any document that initiates, rescinds, or amends a regulation, policy, or procedure that governs the programs, services, or projects of the university.

Calendars, appointment books or programs and scheduling, or itinerary records maintained by staff during business hours that document appointments, itineraries, and other activities of university officials or employees.
News, press releases, marketing files and collateral, or any public relations files maintained or issued by the university. Includes print, electronic, audio, and audiovisual records.

Includes all correspondence and documentation relating to requests for records that are furnished to the public under the Public Information Act (Chapter 552, Government Code). Also includes withdrawn requests.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Includes all correspondence and documentation relating to requests for records that are excepted under the Public Information Act (Chapter 552, Government Code), including records provided to the legislature under section 552.008.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Charts or diagrams that show the structure of the university and the relationships and relative ranks of its departments and job positions.

Plans and records relating to the process of planning new or redefined programs, services or projects of the university that are neither included in nor directly related to other records series in this schedule.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Copies of all proposed, withdrawn, emergency, and adopted rules; open meetings notices; or any other documents required by law to be submitted to the Texas Register.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Drafts of proposed legislation and related correspondence.
Notes or text of speeches, papers, presentations, or reports delivered in conjunction with university work.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Operational strategic plans, which include descriptions of information resources programs, prepared in accordance with Government Code, 2054.095 and 2056.002. Includes working files and related documentation used in creating the final plan.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Biennial narrative reports to the governor and legislature as required by the university’s enabling statutes. May include but is not limited to: annual narrative reports if they are required by statue; working files; and related documentation used in creating final report.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Manuals, guidelines, administrative rules, or similar records distributed internally for the use of employees or externally to the public or those individuals or entities regulated by the university that sets out the rules, policies, and procedures that govern the university’s programs, services, or projects. Includes working files; development document changes; and related documentation.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Reports made to the Office of Attorney General (OAG) on the university's Public Information Act activities, per Government Code, 552.010.
Transcripts and final decisions of hearings conducted as part of the regulatory process, and hearings on proposed rules and changes.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Final agreement described by Government Code, 2009.054(c), associated with a matter conducted under an alternative dispute resolution procedure in which personnel of a state agency participated as a party on the university's behalf.
series_metadata.legal_citation:
Texas Civil Practice
and Remedies Code,
Section 154.071.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Documents any compliance or ethics incidents, the investigation process, and the resolution including corrective actions taken, and follows United States Federal Sentencing Guidelines that mandate the requirements for an effective compliance program, including a mechanism to report allegations of compliance violations, investigations and findings of reported allegations, and conducting assurance activities. May include but is not limited to: allegations reported to the Fraud, Abuse and Privacy hotline; reports tracking activity data from hotline calls; assurance activity documentation; anonymous reporting; and any other related documentation or correspondence.

Documents and supports institutional routine compliance reviews, monitoring activities, and reports. May include but is not limited to: risk assessment reports; policy and procedure updates; training and education materials; compliance tracking logs; and other relevant documentation.

Master versions and inventory of all forms used internally and externally by the university, including subsequent revisions to a university form or any associated design or design modification requests.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: A records retention schedule (i.e. form SLR 105) that identifies the records that are created and maintained by the university, provides the minimum timeframes the records must be retained, and includes instructions for their disposition. May include working files and documentation of certification and approval by the Texas State Library and Archives Commission.

Forms used to track the transmittal of records to/from onsite and offsite storage areas or a transfer of physical and legal custody. Includes RMD 101, Tx-R-5, 306-58- 1, university storage forms, and university archives transfer forms.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Logs or similar records listing departmental records disposed. Information includes agency identification number, records series title, dates of records, date of disposition, and authorized signature.
Documentation used to provide and improve access to records. May include but is not limited to: indexes; lists; registers; guides; and other finding aids.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Records management plans and similar records that establish the policies and procedures under which records and information are managed in the university.
Transmittals and related service documentation (e.g. form RMD 109) for disaster recovery services provided by TSLAC or other entities.

Includes forms used by TSLAC (e.g. form RMD 113) or other entities to establish disaster recovery services, authorize university staff to access the media, and any other related documentation or correspondence.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Records documenting operational and maintenance requirements of computer hardware. May include but is not limited to: operating manuals; hardware/operating system requirements; hardware configurations; equipment control systems; and associated processing files.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Records adequate to specify all technical characteristics necessary for reading or processing electronic records, for making modifications to computer programs, for accessing, retrieving, manipulating, and interpreting data in an automated system, and to allow for their timely, authorized disposition. May include but is not limited to: user guides; system or sub-system definitions; system specifications; input and output specifications; system flow charts; program flow charts; program maintenance logs; change notices; data element dictionaries; file layouts; code books or tables; and other records that explain the meaning, purpose, structure, logical relationships, and origin of the data elements.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Files needed for electronic data audits. May include but is not limited to: files or reports showing transactions accepted, rejected, suspended, and/or processed; history files or tapes; records of updates to application files; and security logs.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_title: Chargeback Records to Data Processing Services Users
series_metadata.series_description: Records documenting the usage, costs, billing, cost recovery, budgeting, and administrative functions of computer usage and data processing services for individual units/departments/divisions at UTHSA.
Forms and logs used to enter and reconcile data sets submitted for processing.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_title: Software Registrations, Warranties, and License Agreements
series_metadata.series_description: Records the registration and licensing of a software application to activate the software for legal use by the end users of a state agency, along with warranties providing that the software will perform in accordance with functional specifications.
Records documenting the request for and response to help desk tickets received by divisions or units, such as information technology.
Biennial information security plan for protecting the security of the university's information.

Adjoint faculty (without salary) appointments. May include but is not limited to: Adjoint Appointment form; president's letter; military letter; justification memo; C.V. documentation; MOU; name affidavit; and criminal background check.

Applications, resumes, transcripts, letters of reference, and similar documents whose submission by candidates for vacant positions is required on the application form, by application procedures, or in the employment advertisement. Records for internal applicants requesting a transfer from one department or area to another may include the transfer request form; employee's skills sheets; and personal resume for each position the employee applies. Includes personnel background information for commissioned and civillian polilce.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Notes, memoranda, or reports relating to the counseling of an employee for work- related, personal, or substance abuse problems. Usually maintained at the supervisory level except in those agencies with counseling staff.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Internal or external announcements or advertisements of job openings, promotions, training programs, or opportunities for overtime.

Includes a contract or agreement regarding the terms and conditions of employment of an individidual and a contract with a vendor for temporary staffing services. Also included faculty, administrative, and professional Memorandums of Appointment. Executed, renewed, or amended on or after September 1, 2015.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Includes a contract or agreement regarding the terms and conditions of employment of an individidual and a contract with a vendor for temporary staffing services. Also included faculty, administrative, and professional Memorandums of Appointment. Executed, renewed, or amended on or before August 31, 2015.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Disciplinary actions are those actions which may affect pay, status, or tenure. They are imposed to discipline an employee whose conduct is harmful to the best interests of the state, the university, or the employee work force; and for failure to improve performance or conduct following imposition of corrective action. May include cause for demotion, suspension, or dismissal, and the reasons for failure to give written notice of resignation. May also be used to document evidence of employee self-improvement efforts, as well as favorable and unfavorable communication.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Forms or similar records used to create or change information concerning the records of an employee. May include but is not limited to: pay grade; position classification; employee number; evaluation date; termination of employment; biographical data sheets; employee separation forms; exit/clearance forms; notifications of intent to hire faculty candidates; position audit documentation; and transactions regarding funding for employees currently on the budget, placing a new employee on the budget, or change in position.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Job descriptions, including all associated task or skill statements, for positions in the university.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Criminal history record information on job applicants or university employees obtained from the Department of Public Safety (DPS).

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Department of Homeland Security/US Citizenship and Immigration Services Form I-9.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Summary of applicant qualifications, evaluation basis for selection or rejection, original applications, job assignments, promotions, separations, layoffs, terminations, compensation records, conditions of work, time records, hours of training provided (separate from labor), minority status, and gender of all selected and rejected applicants.

series_metadata.series_title: Employee Drug Testing and Screening Records—Positive Results and Calibrarion
series_metadata.series_description: Records of employee alcohol test results indicating an alcohol concentration of 0.02 or greater; records of employee verified positive controlled substances test results; documentation of refusals to take required alcohol and/or controlled substances tests; employee evaluation and referrals; calibration documentation; records related to the administration of the alcohol and controlled substances testing programs; reasonable suspicion or post-accident testing decisions; medical explanation for test performance or limits; employer's copy of alcohol test form with results; copy of Controlled Substances Test Chain of Custody Control Form; Medical Review Officer (MRO) documents; notice to report for testing; affidavit signed by the employee stating any prescription drugs or over the counter medication currently taken; final clearance to resume working; and a copy of each annual calendar year summary.
series_metadata.legal_citation:
Drug Free Workplace
Act: (Pub. L. 100-690,
Title V, Subtitle D; 41
U.S.C. 701 et seq.); 49
CFR
382.401(b)(1)(iii); 29
CFR Part 98; 49 CFR
382.403 for
commercial motor
vehicle drivers.

series_metadata.series_title: Employee Drug Testing and Screening Records—Negative Results
series_metadata.series_description: Records of negative and canceled controlled substances test results and alcohol test results with a concentration of less than 0.02. May include but is not limited to: documentation of reasonable suspicion or post-accident testing decisions; medical explanation for the inability to provide adequate breath or urine specimen; employer's copy of alcohol test form with results; copy of Controlled Substances Test Chain of Custody Control Form; Medical Review Officer (MRO) documents; notice to report for testing; affidavit signed by the employee stating any prescription drugs or over the counter medication currently taken; and final clearance to resume working.
Polygraph examinations and results that are administered as part of the employment selection process.

series_metadata.series_title: Reports and Studies (Non- Fiscal) - Human Resources Information System Reports (HRIS)
series_metadata.series_description: Data-driven reports that provide insights into various aspects of the university's workforce. May include but is not limited to: employee demographics; performance; attendance; turnover rate; compensation; benefits; training; and other supporting documentation.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Documentation used to start, modify, or stop all voluntary or required deductions from payroll, including garnishment or other court-ordered attachments.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Payroll records and registers documenting employee earnings, wages, and pay. May include but is not limited to: payroll input records; summary statements; payroll vouchers; payroll detail sheets, and payroll history.
Federal income tax records supporting and including Form 990-T.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Used to make increases or decreases to employees’ gross pay, FICA, retirement, or in the computation of taxes.
Employer’s copy of "Employee's Withholding Exemption Certificate".

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Records defining the wage or salary rate for each position in the university expressed in dollars, grades, or step numbers. May include survey data compiled for the creation of the pay plan.
Records and documentation relating to unemployment compensation claims, including reimbursement of funds disbursed by Texas Workforce Commission (TWC).

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Forms used to deposit employee’s earnings into a specified personal account.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Records documenting the amount of pension or deferred compensation earned by individual employees.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Affirmative action plans for regular employees.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Affirmative action plans for contractors and subcontractors.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Reports providing statistical Information on labor force.
Records relating to review and monitoring of job classifications within the university.
Reports to the university from TWC or its predecessor pertaining to employees.

Any reports compiled by the university on aspects of personnel staffing. Records may include but are not limited to: listings of all staff by program or name; staff hired during a month; detailed listings of employees within its organizational structure; position vacancies; analyses of turnover rates and seasonality of employment; reduction in force reports; and other similar supporting documents.
Aptitude, competency, or skills tests and checklists required of job applicants or of current personnel to qualify for promotion or transfer, including validation records.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Completed aptitude, competency, or skills test papers and checklists of job applicants or of current personnel taking a test to qualify for promotion or transfer.
Includes information used to complete EEO (Equal Employment Opportunity) reports, including Post-Employment EEO forms.
Reports, studies, aggregated or summary data, and similar documentation compiled to monitor and demonstrate compliance with the federal Equal Pay Act.
Diversity and recruitment plans and related workforce analysis.

Used to create employee leave balances, to transfer leave balances when an employee transfers positions, to adjust carry-over balances at end of fiscal year, to correct errors on leave accumulation, and to close out leave accounts on separated employees.
Cumulative leave status report issued each pay cycle and provides employee leave status information for each position.

Records documenting individual employee’s hours worked. May include but is not limited to: work schedules; Flexible Work Arrangement Agreement; Less Than Full Time Worked reports; and other documentation evidencing adherence to or deviation from normal hours for those employees working on fixed schedules.

series_metadata.series_title: Time Off and/or Sick Leave Requests
series_metadata.series_description: Requests and authorizations for vacation, compensatory, sick, professional, sick pool, and the Family and Medical Leave Act (FMLA). May include but is not limited to: employee leave request forms; notices of approval or rejection; certification by medical provider; leave history; dispute records; correspondence; and other supporting documentation.
Records documenting the number of hours transferred in and out of comprehensive sick leave pool.

Records used to verify and authorize expenditures and purchase transactions. May include but is not limited to: claims; invoices; statements; copies of checks and purchase orders; journals, registers, and ledgers; encumbrance, purchase, expenditure and special vouchers; balance sheets and monthly financial reports; itemized billing detail; price quotes; and entries documenting the transfer of funds between an agency's budgets. Also includes records that serve to document disbursements, including canceled checks issued for authorized payments; cash receipts; cash logs; cash counts; refunds; claims for reimbursement to employees for travel and other expenses; Texas Facilities Commission Statements (TFC); and other supporting documentation.
Requests for refunds and payment for claims from patient or 3rd party. Includes patient information and other supporting documentation and correspondence.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Records documenting money owed to or received by the university and its collection or receipt. May include but is not limited to: itemized billing detail; copies or stubs; statements; billing registers; general and subsidiary ledgers; account cards; deposit warrants; cash receipts; cash logs; cash counts; credit card receipts; receipts books; cash transfers; daily cash reports; reconciliations; general journal vouchers; special vouchers; expenditure vouchers; budget entries and transfer of funds between an agency's budgets; Texas Facilities Commission (TFC) statements; and other similar records.

Records documenting itemized billing records for medical and dental services rendered. May include but is not limited to: encounter forms; charges; adjustments; and insurance payments submitted by the university for the purpose of billing patients' insurance companies and agencies.
Periodic reports compiled from information entered into the Uniform Statewide Accounting System (USAS).

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Yearly report compiled from information entered into the Uniform Statewide Accounting System (USAS).

Sufficient records to verify information on unclaimed property previously reported to the State Comptroller showing the name and last known address of the apparent owner of reportable unclaimed property, a brief description of the property, and the balance of each unclaimed account, if appropriate.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Records documenting the internal and external procedural requirements with respect to the accounting department of UTHSA.

Records of accounts deemed uncollectible. May include but is not limited to: write-off authorizations and returned checks; warrants; drafts; payment waivers for prescriptions and co-pays; teaching case waivers; and insurance explanation of benefits (EOB).

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Documentation relating to the captial and fixed assets of the university. May include but is not limited to: appraisals; surveys; descriptions of property; property deeds; the names of grantors and grantees; mortgagers and mortgagees; and other supporting documents reflecting legal title. Also documents institution ownership of vehicles such as cars, vans, trucks, trailers, boats, tractors, and farm vehicles; vehicle or property history cards containing data on initial cost, sequential number logs, and depreciation ledgers; summaries used for capital outlay budgeting; and other documents reflecting legal title. Records also include asset sale, auction, or disposal records of university owned equipment.
Records documenting requests for payment of a sum of money according to the terms of a policy or contract.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Records documenting state, federal, and other sponsored grant projects participated in or administered by state agencies. Includes grant authorization records, which provide evidence of the award of grants to or by agencies; grant/project financial or performance reports, which are periodic reports of financial activity and/or program performance related to grants received or made by agencies. Records may include but are not limited to: award letter/notice; proposal; protocols; correspondence; equipment inventory and final report; memorandums of understanding; debarment certifications; National Institutes of Health (NIH) modular grant internal use documents; human subjects letters to the NIH; Institutional Review Board (IRB) clinical study or sponsored research agreements; Intergovernmental Personnel Act (IPA) Agreements; deposit requests; and forms used to obtain advance approval for payment prior to the contract finalization.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Non-awarded, denied, or unfunded grant applications and proposals, including internal institutional grant programs, departmental research funds, etc. Records may include but are not limited to: correspondence; human subjects letters to NIH (National Institutes of Health); memorandums of understanding; NIH modular grant internal use documents; and other applicable grant documents.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Records documenting financial obligations of UTHSA that are not payable within one year of the date of the balance sheet. Records may include but are not limited to: debentures; loans; deferred tax liabilities; bonds; and pension obligations.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Signature cards or similar records establishing authority of the university employee to initiate or authorize financial transactions on behalf of the university. Also includes Texas Facilities Commission (TFC) authorized representatives records, Inter-Governmental Programs, and Federal Surplus Property Program .

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Logs documenting requests and receipt of FTI, including any information created by the recipient that is derived from federal return or return information received from the IRS or obtained through a secondary source.

Indirect cost plan and supporting documentation created or maintained in the development of the plan. Includes data gathered from departments regarding expenses from a particular budget that cannot be included on an indirect cost proposal, and data gathered from departments about the allocation of staff time for various activities, such as research, instruction, or administration.
Detailed information and worksheets documenting production, job, labor, quotes, pricing, specifications, and other similar records.

Bank statements; credit card statements; electronic fund transfers; cancelled checks; check registers; deposit slips; direct deposit registers; debit and credit notices; currency orders; reconciliations; and other banking related records.
Signature authorizations; active dates of account; modification of account numbers; authorized users/approvers; and related documents and correspondence used to create banking accounts.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Required by the General Appropriations Act. Includes but is not limited to: encumbrances and documentation about budget transfers and revisions; detail charts of accounts; and holds placed on funds for payment of services.

Contracts with insurance companies for reimbursement for patient services. Records may include but are not limited to: credentialing requirements, reimbursement rates, participation terms; and termination and renewal.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Records documenting incoming or outgoing deliveries. May include but are not limited to: private courier services; delivery tracking; and verifying delivery to departments.
Any mailing address, telephone or fax number, or e-mail address records maintained by the university on its employees or on entities or persons it serves.
Records and reports of postage expenses, including postal meter usage.

series_metadata.series_title: Requisitions for In-Agency or Inter-Agency Copy/Printing Service
series_metadata.series_description: Includes word processing and data processing.
Licenses and permits obtained from external agencies or organizations in order to perform operations.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_title: Photocopier and Telefax Usage Logs and Reports
series_metadata.series_description: Registers or logs of print copies and fax transmissions made by user or in total.
Schedules of prices charged by the university for services to the public or other agencies, including any documentation used to determine the charges.

Liability, theft, fire, health, life, automobile, and other policies for government property and personnel including supporting documentation relevant to the implementation, modification, renewal, or replacement of policies. Includes Medical Liability Insurance Ceritficates which provide proof for employee of malpractice insurance coverage. Executed, renewed, or amended on or after September 1, 2015.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Liability, theft, fire, health, life, automobile, and other policies for government property and personnel including supporting documentation relevant to the implementation, modification, renewal, or replacement of policies. Includes Medical Liability Insurance Ceritficates which provide proof for employee of malpractice insurance coverage. Executed, renewed, or amended on or before August 31, 2015.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Any internally distributed manual, guidelines, or similar records that establish standard office procedures for the university; for example, university style manuals, telephone protocols, mail room procedures, bulk mailing permits and procedure, postal box records, and print shop and photocopy ordering instructions.
Any record created by the university to track any type of incoming and outgoing correspondence or packages by the US Postal Service (USPS) or by private couriers.
List of university contracts, leases, and agreements including general obligation, land lease, utilities, and construction contracts.
List of identification codes used to designate buildings at various locations and campuses of the university.
Assessments or evaluations of the value of state-owned buildings or property.

Records documenting building space requests and approvals. Information on request forms may include but is not limited to: the building and room; the reason for the change; parties involved in the request; date of request and approval.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_title: Estimate Files (Supply and Repair Cost Estimates)
series_metadata.series_description: Quotes for minor construction, repair projects, supplies, and canceled work orders/projects. Records include supporting documentation, as applicable.

Requests or work orders for repairs or maintenance to facilities, vehicles, or equipment completed by internal university personnel or external service providers. May include but is not limited to: documents regarding the condition, upkeep, and routine maintenance of the institution's buildings and grounds; and Facilities Management maintenance, renovation, and repair records including billing code, service, labor, parts and remarks.
Any type of usage report or log used to monitor utilities such as gas, electric, water, etc.

Equipment and material descriptions and specifications that may include but are not limited to: detailed descriptions; lists of raw materials and ingredients; physical characteristics of items; special handling procedures; or technical drawings.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Reservation logs or similar records relating to the use of university facilities such as meeting rooms, auditoriums, etc.

UTHSA Facilities Space Planning and Real Estate departmental Space Management System (SMS) records. May include but is not limited to: reports summarizing efficiency of facility space utilization; data on room usage; demand; allocation; capacity; and other documents used for summarizing efficiency of facility space utilization reports.
Documentation of mail returned by the postal or other mail delivery services as undeliverable.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Freight or cargo claims against carrier for damage or loss to a shipment.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Includes bid requisition/authorizations, invitations to bid or propose, bid specifications, awarded and unawarded bids, and bid tabulation/evaluations. Associated with a contract executed, renewed or amended on or after September 1, 2015.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Includes bid requisition/authorizations, invitations to bid or propose, bid specifications, awarded and unawarded bids, and bid tabulation/evaluations. Associated with a contract executed, renewed, or amended on or before August 31, 2015.
Invalid bids that do not meet university submission requirements and are not included in bid evaluation process (e.g., withdrawn, missed submission deadline, incomplete submission, etc.).

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Log, register, etc, providing a record of purchase orders issued, orders received, and similar data on procurement status. May include but is not limited to: sales receipts; packing slips; and invoices.

W-9 IRS Form used to request a taxpayer identification number (TIN) for reporting information to the Internal Revenue Service (IRS). This includes W-9 forms received by the university from vendors, and other records providing vendor profile information.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Inspections conducted in the course of routine business and used by the institution to analyze and correct safety problems. Records may include but are not limited to: inspection sheets with date of inspection, notation of violations, and suggested corrective measures; reports acknowledging compliance with regulations; authorizing signatures; and related information, documentation, and correspondence.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Plans for evacuation of university facilities in cases of emergency.
Plans that provide information to employees about the hazardous chemicals to which they may be exposed to in their workplace.
List of each hazardous chemical normally present in the workplace. Includes chemical inventories.

Logs, registers, or similar records documenting visitors to limited access or restricted areas of university facilities. Also includes student visitors on campus for educational training or collaborative purposes.

Records relating to the issuance of keys, identification cards, building passes, passwords, or similar instruments of access to university facilities, equipment or automated systems. May include but are not limited to: signed statements; access lists; sign-in sheets; logs, registers; instruction books; emergency contact records; denied requests for access; and action records of police personnel opening university facilities for university personnel. Also includes Access Control Executive (ACE) designation forms for departments and records indicating fulfillment of annual ACE training requirements.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Reports of accidents to minors on state property or in any other situation in which the state agency could be a party to a lawsuit.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_title: Select Biological Agents and Toxins Records
series_metadata.series_description: Documents the requirements for possessing, using, or transferring select agents or toxins that might have the potential to pose a severe threat to public health and safety. May include but is not limited to: CDC registration records and application materials; inspection reports; protocols; long term storage inventory records which include name, characteristic, quantity, date of acquisition and source, storage location, movement tracking, agent used, purpose of use, quantity used and by whom, and date, sender, and recipient for intra-entity transfers. If destroyed, records include quantity of toxin destroyed; date and by whom; list of individuals granted access from Health and Human Services; logs and information about entries into areas containing select agents; annual statements documenting eligibility to handle agents; records of theft, loss, release or destruction; discrepancy reports; training records; and records for response including plans.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Includes Radiation Safety Committee minutes and supporting documentation as well as applications to the committee and correspondence between the committee and the faculty authorized user. This records series is also used to document the licensing and registration of the institution by federal, state, and local agencies to receive, use, store, dispose of, and ship radioactive materials and radiation producing machines. These records include federal and state applications and certificates required by the Texas Department of State Health Services - Radiation Control. This series also includes related correspondence and documentation of regulatory agency inspections.

Records document committee overviews of radiation safety, research protocols, scientific and ethical reviews, compliance, and reporting. May include but is not limited to: agendas, meeting minutes, activities, and annual reports submitted to the FDA.

Documents the testing of sealed radioactive sources, and compliance with state, federal, and institutional regulatory standards and envionmental laws. May include but is not limited to: leak test reports; logs or checklists for physical inspections; test procedures; compliance documents; maintenance logs; and other supporting documentation.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_title: Sealed Source Transfers and Disposal Records
series_metadata.series_description: Movement and disposal of sealed radioactive sources, and compliance with state, federal, and institutional regulatory standards and envionmental laws. Records may include but are not limited to: transfer forms and documentation; disposal logs; waste manifests; certificates of disposal or destruction; regulatory compliance records; and other supporting documents.

Identification, location, and status of sealed radioactive sources, and compliance with state, federal, and institutional regulatory standards and envionmental laws. Records may include but are not limited to: reconciliation between inventory logs and physical materials; inventory logs; acquision records; inspection and audit records; usage logs; compliance documentation; and other supporting records.

Documents institutional compliance with environmental laws and guidelines of federal, state, or local governments, including communications with the Regional Air Pollution Authority (RAPA), which is under the jurisdiction of the Environmental Proection Agency (EPA) of the federal government, and also the Texas Commission on Environmental Quality (TCEQ). Records may include but are not limited to: Title V air discharge permits; air quality reports; wastewater discharge permit applications, Public Involvement Plan (PIP) forms; wastewater discharge permits; water quality reports; Slug Control Plan submitted to San Antonio Water System (SAWS); wastewater discharge records; notices of violation; notices of noncompliance; and any other related documentation or correspondence.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_title: Inspection Records for Protective Eyewear for Lasers
series_metadata.series_description: Examinations and documentation to ensure the reliability of eyewear, including protective filters and integrity of the protective filter frames.

Includes records of equipment receipt, transfer, and disposal; registration certificates, conditions or documents incorporated into the license or certification of registration by reference, and amendments thereto; operating procedures applicable to work under the license or certificate of registration; and reports of stolen, lost or missing lasers or IPL devices.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Documentation relating to disputed long distance calls, including documents evidencing repayment by employees for personal long distance use.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Includes mileage, fuel/oil consumption, passengers carried and other related operational information.
Vehicle titles, registration information, and owner manuals for state vehicles.
Records documenting the implementation of programs administered by the office such as marketing products, insurance, and dues information.

Subject files documenting the executive actions of a state university. Records reflect administration of policies, coordination of institution functions, and overall management of major divisions and departments. Records may include but are not limited to: information in the form of correspondence and memoranda; tracking of the handling of inbound mail into the President's Office; safe combination records; policy statements; organization and program development records; reports concerning accreditation requirements; budget material; faculty and student relations; personnel matters; tenure and salary issues; physical plant development; grant awards; research program; foundation endowments; fiscal accountability; academic requirements; annual reports from foundations or from other state agencies; and other related topics.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Periodic performance evaluations and recommendations relating to the review process for tenure for all faculty or staff in the tenure track. May include but is not limited to: faculty appointments; compliance with UT System regulations; appointment, promotion and tenure proposals submitted by department for review by the Promotion, Tenure and Appointment Committee (PTAC); committee recommendations and approvals; and documentation of post tenure review recommendations. Proposals may include Chair's letter of proposal, department or school PTAC committee's letter of recommendation, letters of evaluation; current curriculum vitae; and similar documentation.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_title: International Scholars Records—J-1 Exchange Visitor Files
series_metadata.series_description: Documentation of the short-term appointment of nonimmigrant international scholars as visiting faculty, specialists, researchers, and trainees. May include but is not limited to: Educational Commission for Foreign Medical Graduates (ECFMG) documentation; Form DS-2019; descriptions of work to be performed; methods of financial support; copies of passports; check-in forms; telephone log sheets; and other related documentation.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Includes documentation of an institution’s sponsorship of an applicant using United States Citizenship and Immigration Services (USCIS) Form I-140 (Immigrant Petition for Alien Worker).

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Application and approval of international scholars for permanent immigrant status. May include but is not limited to: Application for Permanent Employment Certifications (Department of Labor (DOL) Form ETA 9089); Application for Alien Employment Certification (DOL Form 750); recruitment reports; copy of job advertisement in national professional journal; and wage determinations.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_title: International Scholars Records—Nonimmigrant Visa Petition Files
series_metadata.series_description: Includes both internal documents and forms submitted and inspected by the U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services (USCIS), including Form I-129, Petition for a Nonimmigrant Worker; and other related materials for H1-B, H-1B1, O-1, TN, and E-3 visas. H-1B Public Inspection File includes but is not limited to: Labor Condition Application; wage rate documentation; summary of benefits; and other mandated H-1B records accessible to the public.

212(n).

series_metadata.series_description: Tax documentation for international students' and scholars' acquisition of social security numbers. May include but is not limited to: social security number applications; statements of information; photocopies of social security cards; and related documentation.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: International exchange visitor selection records, pre-arrival information provided to visitors, program orientation materials, and monitoring of visitors and their dependents.

Documents residency and fellowship application information. May include but is not limited to: AAMC Electronic Residency Application Service (ERAS) documents; common application form; photographs; letters of recommendation; USMLE certified transcript scores; Dean's letter; graduate transcripts; personal statements; ECFMG documents for foreign medical graduates; sanction checks if performed; acknowledgement forms; ranking documentation; rank list to the National Resident Matching Program (NRMP); and evaluations of candidates.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Applications and supplemental materials submitted by individuals, including international students, seeking admission and enrolling in the institution. May include but is not limited to: acceptance letters; applications for admission; entrance exam reports; letters of recommendation; health history forms; readmission forms; recruitment materials; test scores; residency classification forms; academic transcripts; background checks; and related documentation and correspondence.

series_metadata.series_description: Denied applications or applications that were approved but applicant did not enroll in institution or program (includes international applicants). May include but is not limited to: applications for admission; acceptance letters; entrance exam reports; letters of recommendation; health history forms; readmission forms; recruitment materials; test scores; scholarship information; residency classification forms; copies of visa; international student advisors’ notes; degree completion certificates; explanations for student withdrawals; transcripts from other colleges; background checks, and other related documentation and correspondence.

series_metadata.series_description: Documents used to place and remove holds on registration, student services, or the release of transcripts or other academic data until the subject of the hold/encumbrance takes a specific action.

series_metadata.series_description: Departmental approval for students to enroll in independent study courses, including F-1 Student International Exchange visitors. May include but is not limited to: permission sheets with students' names; course names; number of credits; and faculty signatures.

series_metadata.series_description: Academic advising records. May include but is not limited to: advisors' notes; advising checklists; and unofficial or partial copies of grade reports.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Final accepted copies of theses and dissertations presented to colleges in fulfillment of requirements for graduate degrees. May include but is not limited to, copyright disclaimers; listings of schools; reports on final oral examination; requests for final oral examination; recommendations for approval of dissertation/thesis research proposal and supervising committee; notifications of admission to candidacy; and petitions for admission to candidacy.

Transcript, or a record equivalent in function, of the academic achievement of each enrolled student; documenting courses taken; credits granted; grades received; academic honors; awards; and any degrees or certifications awarded (including any narrative assessments or evaluations prepared in lieu of an assigned grade).
Requests and authorizations for a student to withdraw from classes after calendar deadlines without academic penalty for reasons acceptable to a university such as health or family issues.
Tuition and fees accounting records for students who have recieved any type of financial aid.

series_metadata.series_description: Historical reference on the recipients of scholarships in schools or departments. series_metadata.series_description: Denied applications for financial aid and departmental scholarships.

series_metadata.series_description: Approved student applications and disbursement records for Veterans Administration (VA) benefits. May include but is not limited to: financial aid need analysis and eligibility forms; financial aid transcripts; copies of documents submitted by students for verification of student aid application information; receipts; account cards; copies of promissory notes; certifications of enrollment status or other data to grantors; and documentation of entrance and exit interviews.

series_metadata.series_description: Statements by students commenting on contested information in a student record stating why he or she disagrees with a university's decision not to amend a record, or both.

series_metadata.series_description: Requests from students to amend student records, notices by an institution of denial or consent to amendments, requests for hearings on denied requests, hearing notices, and written decisions by hearing examiners.
Student request to opt out of directory information disclosure.

series_metadata.series_description: Reports on student academic standing, including academic performance, progress, deficiency, and the status changes of academically deficient students. Records may include but are not limited to: reports containing student names; date of admission; period of attendance; academic work undertaken; academic work successfully completed; appropriate evaluation of work completed; academic honors, awards, Dean's List; grade point averages (GPAs); grade point deficiencies; numbers of previous suspensions and probations; student petitions for re- evaluation; report of student progress toward academic readmission/removal of probation status at other institutions; and other related documentation or correspondence.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Records documenting procedures performed by dental students. May include, but is not limited to, student grading information and patient treatment information needed to evaluate student.

series_metadata.series_description: Medical and dental clinic reports. May include but is not limited to: Monthly Distribution Report, Credit Balance Report, Aging Analysis Report, Automatic Collection Report, and similar reports generated by university clinics.

Documentation of the professional development, training, contributions, achievements, and career progression of postdoctoral researchers affiliated with UTHSA. May include but is not limited to: appointment letters and employment contracts; onboarding materials; training and certification records; research assignments; mentorship details; performance evaluations; progression reports and any documentation related to the completion of the postdoctoral term.

series_metadata.series_description: Reports written in response to requests from University Hospital Pharmacy and Therapeutics Committee.

Signed authorizations permitting the capture and use of photographs of the patient for medical and dental documentation, treatment planning, potential educational purposes, and/or research. Consent forms must clarify the intended use, distribution, and safeguards to ensure patient confidentiality and compliance with privacy regulations.

series_metadata.series_title: Patient Authorization for Release of Health Records
series_metadata.series_description: Authorizations signed by patients granting permission for the disclosure of their health information to specified individuals, organizations, or entities. May include but is not limited to: the purpose of the release; information to be disclosed; and the expiration of the authorization.

Documentation of abuse and/or neglect findings observed by medical staff during patient care. The findings may be incident/variance reports and evidence of child, domestic, elder, or other kinds of abuse.

series_metadata.series_description: Radiation area surveys performed on patients treated with radioactive materials.
Applications submitted on behalf of a patient to a drug company's or medical institution's assistance program.

United States Food and Drug Administration (FDA) and manufacturer recalls on drugs. May include but is not limited to: reportable occurrences of drug use; recalls through the manufacturer; and responses by departments.

series_metadata.series_description: Final forensic autopsy reports.

series_metadata.series_title: Health Referrals - Denied or Not Seen
series_metadata.series_description: Documentation of instances where outpatients are referred to the institution by non-institutional practitioners who are denied health-related services or who are not seen by the institution.
Lists of potential donors who have been deferred indefinitely due to abnormal test results.

series_metadata.series_description: Documents used in the UT Medicine credentialing process to comply with National Commission on Quality Assurance (NCQA) guidelines and requirements of contracted health plans and state and federal agencies. May include but is not limited to: credentialing applications; audit results; and other relevant records and correspondence.

series_metadata.series_description: All records necessary to fully disclose Medicaid reimbursable services provided, including financial records relating to program services.

series_metadata.series_title: Medical Device Recall Notices and Responses
series_metadata.series_description: United States Food and Drug Administration (FDA), Safe Medical Devices Act (SMDA) and manufacturer recalls on all medical, food and drug devices. May include but is not limited to: reportable occurrences on devices; recalls through the manufacturer; and responses by institutional departments.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Log of patients visiting the institution's health clinics and patient encounters. The log may be used to create annual census reports and 3-year census comparisons, and it may include the date and time that the patient came in; appointment; admission; transport; dispatch; the physician assigned; diagnosis; discharge; patient photo; patient service; length of stay; expired drug disposal; remarks; case logs, and other medical logs.
Records relating to non-forensic autopsies, including reports, blocks, and slides.
Pharmacy log of the unique initials or identification codes which identify pharmacy personnel by name.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Poison Control Center calls and case sheets documenting individual poisoning or suspected poisoning incidents. Information may include date/time called, location of call, case number, name of individual, substance ingested, treatment, and medical outcome.

Quality assurance records such as documentation of equipment monitoring; checks of quality control items, and any necessary corrections; reports by the staff; quality assurance committee notes; Morbidity & Mortality (M&M) conferences for residents; peer reviews of clinical operations, chart reviews; Institutional Review Board (IRB) member and alternate CVs; and staff reviews.

University copies of information relating to the selection by students of health insurance offered to students through UTHSA, and health insurance waivers documenting approval to opt out of university offered insurance options.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: University copies of information relating to liability insurance offered to students. Records may include but are not limited to: list of students that have been charged the liability premiums; documentation stating that students are covered by the blanket liability policies; and any other related documentation or correspondence.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Sterilization of surgical instruments used by the health institution. May include autoclave recording charts and log sheets indicating date, load number, items sterilized, and temperature/time settings

Exhibit records documenting the display and use of artifacts and materials. May include but not limited to: artifact labels or placards; photographs of exhibits; exhibit renderings; and exhibit assembly and presentation instructions.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Documents UTHSA-affiliated patron accounts in the library management system.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Records of the repository granting/denying permission to reproduce images of items in the collection. Includes permission forms and correspondence requesting permission to publish images from archival collections.
Documents campus police response to requests for vehicle assistance such as jumper cable service, unlocking doors, or other similar instance.

Records documenting the monitoring, inspection, measurement, repair, and illumination of antenna, antenna supporting structures, and transmitters authorized to operate with a power output in excess of two watts.

Documents offenses that occur on the university campus that violate state and/or local laws and regulations and involve juvenile offenders adjudicated for delinquent conduct (Class A or B misdemeanor or any felony). May include but is not limited to: case number; type of report; date; time; complainant or victim; address; and other relevant details of report.

34 CFR 668.49; Texas

series_metadata.series_description: Registrations completed by institution students, faculty, and staff for use of bicycles on campus.

Emergency notifications distributed to the campus community via email, text message, or other method regarding emergencies, evacuations, "timely warnings" related to public safety, and weather related instructions. Records include, but are not limited to: sign up records; requests to not participate; test logs; and feedback on tests.
Logs, test records, operational check lists, and similar records relating to the administration of chemical breath tests or breathalyzer.
Copies of child abuse reports received by a law enforcement agency pursuant to Family Code, Sections 261.103 and 261.105, which do not become part of arrest and offense investigation records.

Annual security report summarizing statistics on the nature, date, time, general location, and disposition of the complaint (if known) of crimes investigated by campus police, created pursuant to the Clery Act.

series_metadata.series_title: Clery Act Reporting—Crime Log and Statistics
series_metadata.series_description: Crime log and statistics on criminal homicide; sex offenses; robbery; aggravated assault; burglary; motor vehicle theft; arson; and arrests for liquor law violations, drug law violations, and illegal weapons possession. Includes crimes occurring on campus, in or on non- campus buildings or property, and on public property, pursuant to the Clery Act.

Records created and maintained to anticipate, prevent, or monitor possible criminal activity, including crime pattern, crime analysis, and modus operandi reports; forecasts; evaluation reports; investigation recommendations; reports on movements of known offenders; information on confidential informants; and messages and alerts from other agencies.

Daily routine informational communications for public safety officers and personnel on duty which may include daily blotter, weather advisories, hazardous conditions, and reminders about "be on the lookout for" (BOLO) notifications.
Reports submitted by the director of a law enforcement agency to the Attorney General concerning the deaths of persons while in the custody of a peace officer.

Record created by dispatcher on each call for service documenting the dispatch of a campus police officer to investigate a call for assistance, disturbance, or possible crime on the institution's campus. Includes date and time call received, nature of call, and details of action taken in response to call.
Emergency protective orders issued by magistrates.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Reports on persons stopped and interrogated in the field because of suspicious behavior and/or non-criminal incidents.

Records documenting the initiation, investigation, and disposition of internal affairs investigations of alleged misconduct by law enforcement officers. Includes records of investigation of law enforcement shooting incidents which result in death or injury to any person, including a police officer.

Records documenting the initiation, investigation, and disposition of internal affairs investigations of alleged misconduct by law enforcement officers. Includes records of investigations that result in sustained formal discipline (i.e., disciplinary action at or above the level of a written reprimand).

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Records documenting the initiation, investigation, and disposition of internal affairs investigations of alleged misconduct by law enforcement officers. Includes records of investigations that result in sustained informal discipline (i.e., disciplinary action below the level of a written reprimand) or of investigations whose findings are inconclusive.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_title: Internal Affairs Investigation Records— Unfounded/Not Sustained
series_metadata.series_description: Records documenting the initiation, investigation, and disposition of internal affairs investigations of alleged misconduct by law enforcement officers. Includes records of investigations whose findings are not sustained, or in cases where accusations are determined to be unfounded or the accused is exonerated.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Reports on missing children and adults, requests for investigation, photographs, fingerprints, dental records, X-rays, notifications of possible match, and similar documents relating to the location of missing children and adults, including similar records relating to unidentified bodies.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Photographs or mug shots of known offenders used by crime victims or witnesses for identification, other than those maintained in arrest records and offense investigations.

series_metadata.series_title: Offense Records—Class A and B Misdemeanors and State Jail Felonies
series_metadata.series_description: Cases cleared by the conviction or acquittal of the person arrested or cited, by the dismissal of charges against the person, or by the entry on the record of a court by a prosecuting attorney of a nolle prosequi. May include but is not limited to: offense and supplemental offense reports containing case number, type of report, date, time, complainant or victim, and address; investigation reports and notes; witness statements; latent fingerprints; results of chemical analysis and polygraph tests; crime scene, mug shot, and other photographs; laboratory reports; citations; affidavits; criminal process; victim impact statements; and other records relating and customary to the investigation of criminal offenses or other violations of state law or local ordinance.

Arrest reports and citations for Class C misdemeanors and unclassified violations of state law or local ordinance punishable by fine only, such as Minor in Possession (MIP), Minor in Consumption (MIC), and possession of drug paraphernalia.

series_metadata.series_title: Offense Records—First- Degree and Capital Felonies
series_metadata.series_description: Cases cleared by the conviction or acquittal of the person arrested or cited, by the dismissal of charges against the person, or by the entry on the record of a court by a prosecuting attorney of a nolle prosequi. May include but is not limited to: offense and supplemental offense reports; investigation reports and notes; witness statements; latent fingerprints; results of chemical analysis and polygraph tests; crime scene, mug shot, and other photographs; laboratory reports; citations; affidavits; criminal process; victim impact statements; and other records relating and customary to the investigation of criminal offenses or other violations of state law or local ordinance.

Documents cases cleared by the conviction or acquittal of the person arrested or cited, by the dismissal of charges against the person, or by the entry on the record of a court by a prosecuting attorney of a nolle prosequi. May include but is not limited to: detailed offense and supplemental offense reports; investigation reports and notes; witness statements; latent fingerprints; results of chemical analysis and polygraph tests; crime scene, mug shot, and other photographs; laboratory reports; citations; affidavits; criminal process; victim impact statements; and other records relating and customary to the investigation of criminal offenses or other violations of state law or local ordinance.
Information supplied by pawn shops and sent to law enforcement. Includes individual receipts or lists of items pawned.

Logs or equivalent records, usually arranged chronologically or by case, court, or citation number, providing summary data on police activities. Activities may include but are not limited to: complaints investigated by law enforcement officers; arrests made; citations issued; accidents investigated, including accidents involving vehicles owned by the university; vehicle accident claims; court arraignments; court appearances by officers; and other similar activities relating to the arrest or citation of persons or the investigation of offenses.
Audiovisual recordings, including those from police vehicles, body worn cameras, or digital cameras, of persons detained for possible Driving While Intoxicated (DWI) or other violations.

series_metadata.series_description: Rap sheets received from the Texas Department of Public Safety (DPS) or other law enforcement agencies.
Informational forms or lists provided to officers for campus areas and buildings needing special watch, including requests from the public for such watches.
Records received on persons wanted by other law enforcement agencies.
Records documenting the disposition of police weapons, including but not limited to buy-back programs, trade-ins, and upgrades with vendors.
Records of inspection and repair of weapons.
Inventories of weapons.
Logs for issuing weapons to officers.

series_metadata.series_description: Records used to assist students in planning career goals and connecting with prospective employers. series_metadata.series_description: Residency program curriculum records. May include but is not limited to: schedules of conferences; curriculum used in residency programs; and attendance sheets for conferences and special programs.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Records documenting the administration of academic programs, special academic programs, or non-institution student programs of a department or college. May include but is not limited to: application, admission, and non-acceptance documentation; health information; recommendations and evaluations; copies of academic records; advising notes; risk release and insurance forms; immigration and citizenship documentation; financial responsibility records; registration reports; add-drop analyses and reports; course enrollment summaries by class; graduation summaries; international activities; cooperative ventures; summer term classes and enrollment reports; placement information; and working papers.

Records documenting the institution's participation in cooperative and shared educational or research programs. Such programs may share research facilities and resources or instructional programs. May include but is not limited to: information on requirements and application procedures; committee minutes; meeting agendas; affiliation agreements; and memos of interpretation and understanding.

series_metadata.series_title: Degree and Special Academic Program Proposal, Development and Review Records
series_metadata.series_description: Records documenting planning, development, approval, and discussions relating to the implementation of new undergraduate, advanced degree, and special academic programs and any major reorganization or changes to established programs. May include but is not limited to: final reports; curriculum committee meeting minutes; curriculum proposals; reviews of individual degree programs by campus and off-campus sources; letters of support; review agendas; and related documentation.
