series_metadata.series_description: Accreditation records for residency programs. May include but is not limited to: correspondence to and from OGME; internal reviews by GMEC (Graduate Medical Education committee); correspondence by GMEC regarding internal reviews and action plans developed to correct citations; correspondence with GMEC regarding action plans; and program Effectiveness Committee minutes.
Meeting agenda, minutes, notes, and other related records documenting the activities of the University Research Council.

Records supporting Material Transfer Agreements (MTAs) - legal contracts that govern the transfer of tangible research materials and intellectual property between institutions. Information may include but is not limited to: ownership; usage rights and restrictions; confidentiality documents; liability and risk documents; publication rights; compliance; and termination.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Information provided by faculty on conflicts of interest or commitment that might impair objectivity when designing, conducting, or reporting research or other scholarly activity. Includes Conflict of Interest forms.

Contracts or written agreements between the university and organizations or entities outlining the terms and conditions for collaboration on federal and non-federal grant proposals or other special projects. May include but is not limited to: roles; responsibilities; and resource-sharing arrangements.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Background information and other documents used in support of consulting services provided by faculty for Medical Service Research Development Plan (MSRDP) and Dental Service Research and Development Plan (DSRDP).
Medical records for care of privately owned animals treated at the university. Does not include research animals owned by the university.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Records relating to the care, management, and breeding of animals for research and teaching purposes. May include but is not limited to: investigator; vendor; protocol; location information; animal cage cards; research animal room maintenance records that report housing, cleaning, disinfection, ventilation, feed, water, cages, air filters, and daily health checks; disposition or transport of animals forms including, Animal Plant and Health Inspection Services (APHIS) Form 7020, Laboratory Animal Resources Request for Transfer of Experimantal Animals form; and Certificate of Veterinary Inspection.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_title: Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC) Records—Assurances
series_metadata.series_description: Formal agreements submitted to and approved by the Public Health Service (PHS) to document compliance with federal regulations, policies, and ethical standards for the care and use of animals in research.
Documents meeting agendas, minutes, activities, and reports of the UTHSA Institutional Biosafety Committee and the UT System Institutional Biosafety Committee.

series_metadata.series_title: Institutionally Funded Research—Minutes and Final Research Reports
series_metadata.series_description: Documents the activities of the institutional councils and boards, which review proposals and make recommendations for awards to faculty for research that is not otherwise supported by organized or directed programs but is designed to lead to other funding sources. Examples of projects funded are pilot research, emergency funding, emerging research opportunities, new research field or new research field for investigator, developing research laboratories, and centrally shared research resources. This series includes meeting minutes and final research reports.

Documents the activities of the institutional councils and boards, which review proposals and project funding requirements to make recommendations for awards to faculty for research that is not otherwise supported by organized or directed programs but is designed to lead to other funding sources. Examples of projects funded are pilot research, emergency funding, emerging research opportunities, new research field or new research field for investigator, developing research laboratories, and centrally shared research resources. May include but is not limited to: applicant case files; agendas; notes; working papers; award letters; and applications for research support.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Documents the transfer of technology from the institution to outside agencies as the result of research projects and grants carried out at the institution. May include but is not limited to: patent applications; agreements giving permission for institutional researchers to use other patented inventions in their research; descriptions and titles of inventions; sources of funding to create the inventions; details of the provenance of the inventions and their documentation; suggested manufacturers; reports issued concerning the inventions; and signatures of inventors and technically qualified witnesses. Also includes all other agreements not associated with a specific invention report form, patent, trademark or copyright, and other related correspondence and financial documentation.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_title: Patent and Invention Records—Denied or Not Pursued
series_metadata.series_description: Records relating to disclosures or applications for patents that are either not filed or otherwise pursued by the university, or which are denied by the US Patent and Trademark Office (USPTO).

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Records documenting ownership, rights, licensing, and obligations of employee inventions developed using federal funding to ensure compliance with Bayh- Dole Act requirements.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Final and interim research reports, working files, research data, protocol and amendments, laboratory notebooks, and any specimens produced for a granting agency or sponsor for funded research, studies, clinical trials and non-clinical trials. May include but is not limited to: study objectives, purpose, and design; selection criteria; participant records; case history records and reports; human research protocols; human subjects letters to National Institutes of Health (NIH); memorandums of understanding; Institutional Review Board (IRB) clinical study or sponsored research agreements; patient care data; clinical procedures; pharmaceutical studies; Food and Drug Administration (FDA) forms; serious adverse events reports; canceled drug studies; Office of Sponsored Programs deposit requests; findings; non-medical findings; research papers; and drug/device accountability reports (DAR).

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Accusations of research project-related misconduct brought forward by or against university personnel and affiliates. May include but is not limited to: written reports; all statements; data; other evidence considered during the inquiry and investigation; records of terminated principal investigators; correspondence related to safety issues; surveys performed in their laboratories; spills/injuries that may have occurred; and minutes of Conflict of Interest Committee.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Documents the history, development, and policies of campus student sponsored organizations. May include but is not limited to: constitutions and bylaws; publications (websites, newsletters, fliers, brochures, posters, and other publications); annual review forms; annual reports; meeting minutes and supporting documentation; committee, subcommittee, and task-force records; Student Senate bill and resolution files; budgets; handbooks; officer and member rosters; scrapbooks; photographs; press releases; clippings; social media accounts; and related documentation and correspondence that documents programs, activities, and events.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Records documenting the history, development, and policies of UTHSA sponsored student organizations. May include but is not limited to: constitutions and bylaws; publications (websites, newsletters, fliers, brochures, posters, and other publications); annual review forms; annual reports; meeting minutes and supporting documentation; committee, subcommittee, and task-force records; Student Senate bill and resolution files; budgets; handbooks; officer and member rosters; scrapbooks; photographs; press releases; clippings; social media accounts; and related documentation and correspondence that documents programs, activities, and events.
Records of elections held by various faculty and staff organizations. May include but is not limited to: ballots; tabulations; and related documentation.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
